title: Bridge Essay: Intellectual cyborgs and the future of science.
concept: Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Mind as Computer Critique** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** 4E cognition — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended mind; critique of computationalism.
**Phase context:** Mind not a computer — embodied, embedded, enactive, extended cognition.

**Intellectual cyborgs and the future of science.** — Trends Cogn Sci (2023). DOI: 10.1016/j.tics.2023.06.004. PMID: 37442679. Unlike classical artificial intelligence (AI), generative AI has the potential to transform scientists into intellectual cyborgs. Leveraging embodied cognition and extended mind theories can help us understand this scientific revolution. Despite ethical concerns, generative AI can enhance research efficiency and accessibility. However, this requires unprecedented proactive regulation and responsible development.

notes: Auto-acquired from PubMed/MEDLINE on 2026-07-12. Phase 3 (Mind as Computer Critique). Evaluation: Good (score=3.0)
